issue_title: z3 4.5.0 incorrectly returns unsat for model
issue_body:
See the attached zip file for an SMT file that reproduces the problem; this was tested in z3 4.4.1 (returns sat, and the correct model) and z3 4.5.0 (returns unsat) using the 64 bit windows binaries available here. The bug arose in testing of the BMA (https://github.com/Microsoft/BioModelAnalyzer)

The model has three variables, vA vB and vO. All three are integers, constrained to be equal to 1 through simple assertions. O is also constrained to be 1 by a formula dependent on the values of A and B converted to reals. 

In testing with z3 4.5.0 Deletion of either assertion that constrains vO causes z3 to return sat and the correct model (vA=1,vB=1,vO=1), but with both assertions present the model returns unsat. Additionally, replacing instances of vA or vB with 1 in the complex constraint on vO causes z3 to return sat.

[problem.zip](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/965002/problem.zip)

issue_title: aiohttp 2.0.x regression on timeouts (proxy combined with throttling through Semaphore?)
issue_body:
## Long story short

Exactly the same code runs on aiohttp 1.3.x and fails in 2.0.5

## Expected behaviour

I use async.gather() to create 1000 futures occurence of an API call, where timeout is set to 5 secs.
I use a semaphore to limit to 100 the amount of parallel running requests.
When running on 2.x it works if I do not use a proxy, but it fails if I define a proxy.
The whole process should take around 20s and does so on aiohttp 1.3.5

## Actual behaviour

After 5 seconds, the requests are failing due to TimeoutError. I fear the proxy connections of the futures are started (aenter) all at once, not when the semaphore actually releases them and thus a TimeoutError is probably raised before the requests were actually sent out.

## Steps to reproduce

I've tried to describe it above. My code is actually pretty complex, but as I said a simple downgrade of aiohttp fixes the problem.
Maybe the description is enough to fix the bug. Else, if required, I could try to write a proper test, but this is not so tricky as it requires finding real-life API calls to be made to trigger the timeouts... Maybe the description is enough...

## Your environment

Ubuntu 16.04, python 3.6.0 or 3.5.1, pyenv virtualenv and proxy in local network

issue_title: Sharing the event loop
issue_body:
I would like to use aiohttp to make a python program that can:

* act as webserver
* act as webserver with websockets
* act as telnet server
* act as telnet client
* offer a CLI interface https://github.com/jonathanslenders/python-prompt-toolkit/blob/2.0/docs/pages/advanced_topics/asyncio.rst https://github.com/jonathanslenders/python-prompt-toolkit/blob/2.0/examples/prompts/asyncio-prompt.py

Because i want to monitor (and use the web to visualize this) and inject traffic on the telnet connection (this python program sits in between two other programs). For telnet i would like to use this library https://github.com/jquast/telnetlib3 which also works with asyncio. I've searched the documentation of aiohttp to see if i can do something with the event loop directly and found this page https://docs.aiohttp.org/en/stable/web_lowlevel.html However it does not fit my use case looking by the first line: `Sometimes user don’t need high-level concepts introduced in Server Usage: applications, routers, middlewares and signals.`. I DO want all the high-level concepts and use routers, middleware and signals for all my http related stuff. I just like to mix in telnet as well. My goal is stream values between telnet and websocket. How can i have access to the low-level event loop but keep all the high-level good stuff that aiohttp gives me? I would also like to prevent aiohttp from blocking the terminal so i can provide a CLI interface with python-prompt-toolkit.

issue_title: Broken algorithm for generating block header numbers
issue_labels: [{'id': 815420353, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw4MTU0MjAzNTM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/comp:%20trinity', 'name': 'comp: trinity', 'color': 'bfd4f2', 'default': False}, {'id': 983629570, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjk1NzA=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/effort:%20E1%20hours', 'name': 'effort: E1 hours', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}, {'id': 983627565, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjc1NjU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/prio:%20P2%20required', 'name': 'prio: P2 required', 'color': 'f7c6c7', 'default': False}, {'id': 630871939, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw2MzA4NzE5Mzk=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'eeeeee', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/3dbad6b8ea82b2f325b3fce6d96a7f0dc2b5642f/p2p/chain.py#L775-L782

The algorithm linked above and included here is not generating block numbers according to protocol.

```python
if reverse:
    low = max(0, block_number - limit)
    high = block_number + 1
    block_numbers = reversed(range(low, high, step))
else:
    low = block_number
    high = block_number + limit
block_numbers = range(low, high, step)
```

For example, if requesting `5` headers starting at block number `0`, with a skip of `1`:

- expected correct: `0, 2, 4, 6, 8`
- actual: `0, 2, 4` (doesn't return 5 headers)

### How can it be fixed

Need to fix how the limits are computed to take into account the skip size so that we still return the appropriate number of headers.

issue_title: fetch_insights_async() incorrectly prints warnings about unsupported fields
issue_body:
This is due to the fact that FacebookRequest._accepted_fields is inferred from the target class.  See https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/api.py#L581

For insights_async requests, the target class is AdReportRun, as expected.  However, in this case, the _accepted_fields should not be inferred from AdReportRun, but rather from AdInsights.

issue_title: Enable multi-key match in AdAccount.opt_out_user_from_targeting()
issue_body:
As there is no opportunity to set `is_raw` or `pre_hashed` when calling [`CustomAudience.format_params()`](https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/7be87a805067c7e0d1ff004e40cb9ba8a987e206/facebookads/adobjects/helpers/customaudiencemixin.py#L53)from within [`AdAccount.opt_out_user_from_targeting()`](https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/adobjects/helpers/adaccountmixin.py#L65) usage is limited to removing users by one of `UID`, `MOBILE_ADVERTISER_ID`, `EMAIL_SHA256` or `PHONE_SHA256`. Whilst also passing non-hashed email or phone.

According to [Updating Custom Audience](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/reference/custom-audience/#Updating) and [Updating Custom Audience Users](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/reference/custom-audience/users/#Updating) the endpoint (`https://graph.facebook.com/<API_VERSION>/act_<AD_ACCOUNT_ID>/usersofanyaudience`) supports multi-key insert. 

It would be usefull to remove users if they opt out in the same way we are adding them. e.g.

```python
from facebookads.adobjects.adaccount import AdAccount

adaccount = AdAccount('<AD_ACCOUNT_ID>')
users = [['FirstName', 'test2@example.com', 'LastName1'],
         ['FirstNameTest', 'test2@example.com', 'LastNameTest']]

schema = [CustomAudience.Schema.MultiKeySchema.fn,
          CustomAudience.Schema.MultiKeySchema.email,
          CustomAudience.Schema.MultiKeySchema.ln]

adaccount.opt_out_user_from_targeting(schema, users, is_raw=True)
```

issue_title: HMM used for Bayesian Knowledge Tracing
issue_body:
Need suggestions on HMM used for BKT :) 

As we know BKT (Bayesian Knowledge tracing) is essentially a two-state HMM but has its specials. 

1) If we define two states as learned and not-learned. The transaction matrix is defined as :[ [ 1-forget, forget],[learn, 1-learn]]. Here comes the first special point: we have to force forget=0, meaning never be learned to not-learned or in other words, never forget

2) Suppose the observations have also two states , right or wrong, We can define our emission matrix as following [[1- slip, slip],[guess, 1-guess]]. Here comes the second special point: we empirically force guess<0.3 and slip<0.1.

My question is how to setup these limitations in HMMlearn? I know by default, all three matrix will be trained. 

Second question is how to change training starting point? I know EM method is typically local optimization, so we need to randomize starting point but I didn't find where to input those numbers. 

Thanks, Qintao

issue_title: Can't override `type="search"` for text field anymore
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm migrating my project from Django 1.9 to 1.11. I have the following code which was working great in Django 1.9 but doesn't work in Django 1.11:

```html
{% render_field form.q type='search' required='required' class='term-input' %}
```

And here's my Django form:

```py
class SearchForm(forms.Form):
    q = forms.CharField(label='Search')
```

Only if I explicitly override the field widget's `type` atribute - it works:

```py
class SearchForm(forms.Form):
    q = forms.CharField(label='Search', widget=TextInput(attrs={'type': 'search'}))
```

## Investigation

AFAIU, Django 1.11 has moved widgets rendering from Python code into templates (which is 100% positive change IMO), but now they treat `type` attribute in a special way:

```
{# django-1.11.7/django/forms/templates/django/forms/widgets/input.html #}
<input type="{{ widget.type }}" name="{{ widget.name }}"{% if widget.value != None %} value="{{ widget.value|stringformat:'s' }}"{% endif %}{% include "django/forms/widgets/attrs.html" %} />
```

So when I override `{% render_field form.q required='required' class='term-input' data-x=1 %}` - everything works great, but not with `type=search'` :(

issue_title: Incorrect documentation for people_track_charge(...)
issue_body:
The current version of mixpanel on pypi is 4.0.2. at the time of writing.

The documentation (http://mixpanel.github.io/mixpanel-python/#mixpanel.Mixpanel.people_track_charge) depicts the behavior for 4.0.2+ though since the handling of the properties=None changed there (https://github.com/mixpanel/mixpanel-python/commit/d92dd5faf69f3a743c093396e8699f6747efbadf).

Possible solutions:
1. Update the documentation and change the function signature to: people_track_charge(distinct_id, amount, properties, meta=None)
OR
2. Release version 4.0.3.

issue_title: Cannot detect Windows Arrow Keys
issue_body:
Hi there,

Having problems sending arrow key events in Command.exe on Windows 10. I am using a serial connection to a Micropython board ( https://vgkits.org/blog/2018/05/08/introductory-video-tutorial/ ) as a worked example.

Not sure if this is a different issue to https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/issues/207 as the modified version of miniterm indicated in that thread doesn't seem to resolve the issue.

On Mac and Linux, miniterm seems to be fine, launched as documented here...
https://vgkits.org/blog/connecting-with-miniterm/
...and the arrow keys work fine on Mac and Linux too.

However, the Windows invocation documented there creates a console session in which arrow keys and backspace keys are junked.

The invocation I tried most recently on Windows (note this was an attempt to use the 'patched miniterm-MPY') was...

```bash
python miniterm_mpy.py --raw --eol CRLF --encoding ascii COM3 115200
```

This leads to a terminal in which pressing the up key produces first...

```
?H?
```

...and then if you press again it seems to send an up arrow key and shows...

```
 clearPixel()
```

...then if you press again it shows 

```
 clearPixel()[12DsetPixel(0, red)
```
...and finally...
```
 clearPixel()[12DsetPixel(0, red)[16D[K4+4
```

What it should show is single, clear lines drawn from the history one by one as you press up and down like...

```
clearPixel()
setPixel(0, red)
4+4
```

I'd really like to know that I don't have to guide users to run everything inside the Ubuntu subsystem on Windows 10 just to get a terminal to work, but it sounds like that might be true.

issue_title: Capture Request Context
issue_body:
Our Ruby agent [allows](http://help.apm.scoutapp.com/#ruby-custom-context) capturing arbitrary key/value pairs to identify a transaction. We should extend this agent to do the same.

I'm not sure of the best API for this yet. but it'll likely be very similar to the Ruby one, with a class that you call methods on, which then coordinates the mechanism for persisting that data.

Note: Once this is done, we should also add automatic capturing of user and url information. (IP, Logged in user's username, the full request url and so on).

issue_title: using b2 command line tool as a library
issue_body:
I would like to implement something in python using b2 and its command line tool. Current implementation already lets me import it (after I symlink it to `b2.py`, that is) and call functions on it, which is good, however for any application which is not console-based or requires some resiliency against errors, it is impossible to use due to the current architecture. It is also not possible to test it automatically in isolation. Clearly the current codebase is already designed with integration with other python code - at least partially.

The problem is with two layers being mixed with each other. One layer is what performs operations on the backend (let's call it controller) and the other is what displays the retrieved information (let's call it view). For example, if I would like to create a GUI application which would present the result of `ls`, I can't, because the function uses `print` to return results to the user. Here the layers should be divided so that the backend function returns an iterator of entries (objects), which would then be printed by the view function. If someone would need to call a library equivalent of `ls`, he would call the backend one and then display the result in the GUI (or web page or whatever he is trying to build).

Another example is that if something does not exist or there is some other problem, current tool calls `sys.exit()`. If the user supplies invalid data to the gui application, as an application developer I want to display an error message, not shutdown the whole program. Therefore I'd expect the library functions to raise an exception on error, so that it can be handled properly.

It is possible to implement most of the functionality of b2 command line tool again in a way which has properly separated layers and allows for console, gui, web page and other types of outputs, as well as for automated testing in isolation.

On the other hand, the same changes could be developed here in this repository, with keeping most of the interface as it is. To be precise, I'd contribute most (if not all) of the code and tests required to do this properly. As it is a larger amount of work, I'd like to discuss it first here, so if my view and views of official maintainers are not aligned, it can be compared before much work is put into development.

Please comment the above.

issue_title: New feature: tortuosity
issue_body:
It would be useful for validations to add the section_tortuosity feature at the "get" level for the next release. 

Definition of tortuosity can be found here (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3928584/):

"Also, the distance metric (DM) tortuosity (a dimensionless number) provides a ratio between the actual path length `(correction: this should be section length)` of a meandering curve (i.e. along segments) and the linear distance between endpoints (radial distance)"

Neuroanatomical Tract-Tracing: Molecules, Neurons, and Systems, by Laszlo Zaborszky, Floris G. Wouterlood, José Luis Lanciego

> The dendritic tortuosity gives a ratio between the actual length of a dendritic segment (our section) and the distance between its endpoints

issue_title: API Keys
issue_body:
Caveat to this issue: I maybe misreading the code and there is another way to do this that I am not seeing.

While working on the Java SDK I see this project and PHP SDK require the API Key to be set inside a checked in file. [Example](https://github.com/SparkPost/python-sparkpost/blob/master/examples/transmissions/send_transmission.py#L3)

I believe things like that should not be placed inside checked in code because it is too easy to accidentally checkin you key.

I would prefer to see a name something like "config.something.example" that gets checked in. Put "config.something" would be put in the .gitignore so ti is not checked in. The example file would have the place holder properties.

The above filenames should use something appropriate for the language. In Java it would probably be "config.properties.example".

issue_title: don't just ignore oversized stack frame local in get_frame_locals
issue_body:
```
def get_frame_locals(frame, transformer=transform, max_var_size=4096):
...
    for k, v in iteritems(f_locals):
        v = transformer(v)
        v_size = len(repr(v))
        if v_size + f_size < 4096:
            f_vars[k] = v
            f_size += v_size
```

That will just leave out the oversized variable WITHOUT ANY NOTICE. Come on! If it's oversized at least add ```f_vars[k] = "<Value omitted because it's too big>"```. Otherwise I can debug all day why it's missing.

BTW, the `max_var_size` parameter seems to be a decoy here.

issue_title: Django 1.9 exception if contrib apps are not loaded
issue_body:
In Django 1.9 it became an error to import a model of an app before it is loaded (https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.9/releases/1.9/#features-removed-in-1-9):

>All models need to be defined inside an installed application or declare an explicit app_label. Furthermore, it isn’t possible to import them before their application is loaded. In particular, it isn’t possible to import models inside the root package of an application.

If you are running a lean django setup without users / content types the import of AbstractBaseUser / BaseUser triggers this condition and raven explodes.

````
Unable to process log entry: Model class django.contrib.contenttypes.models.ContentType doesn't declare an explicit app_label and either isn't in an application in INSTALLED_APPS or else was imported before its application was loaded. 
Traceback (most recent call last):

    client.captureException(exc_info=sys.exc_info(), request=request)
  File "/home/kieran/.virtualenvs/grizzly-services/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/raven/base.py", line 698, in captureException
    'raven.events.Exception', exc_info=exc_info, **kwargs)
  File "/home/kieran/.virtualenvs/grizzly-services/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/raven/contrib/django/client.py", line 142, in capture
    data.update(self.get_data_from_request(request))
  File "/home/kieran/.virtualenvs/grizzly-services/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/raven/contrib/django/client.py", line 56, in get_data_from_request
    from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractBaseUser as BaseUser
  File "/home/kieran/.virtualenvs/grizzly-services/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/contrib/auth/models.py", line 6, in <module>
    from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType
  File "/home/kieran/.virtualenvs/grizzly-services/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/contrib/contenttypes/models.py", line 159, in <module>
    class ContentType(models.Model):
  File "/home/kieran/.virtualenvs/grizzly-services/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/models/base.py", line 103, in __new__
    "application was loaded. " % (module, name))
RuntimeError: Model class django.contrib.contenttypes.models.ContentType doesn't declare an explicit app_label and either isn't in an application in INSTALLED_APPS or else was imported before its application was loaded.

issue_title: Cannot use BatchJob to update labels
issue_body:
I'm trying to change a campaignLabel using the BatchJob.
Here https://developers.google.com/adwords/api/docs/guides/batch-jobs#supported_operations  is says that CampaignLabel is a supported operation.

Unfortunately this 
`````
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<SOAP-ENV:Envelope xmlns:SOAP-ENV="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:ns0="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:ns1="https://adwords.google.com/api/adwords/cm/v201601" xmlns:tns="https://adwords.google.com/api/adwords/cm/v201601" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
   <SOAP-ENV:Header>
      <tns:RequestHeader>
         <tns:clientCustomerId>XXX-XXX-XXXX</tns:clientCustomerId>
         <tns:developerToken>1234567890</tns:developerToken>
         <tns:userAgent>Webrepublic..</tns:userAgent>
         <tns:validateOnly>false</tns:validateOnly>
         <tns:partialFailure>false</tns:partialFailure>
      </tns:RequestHeader>
   </SOAP-ENV:Header>
   <ns0:Body>
      <ns1:mutateLabel>   <----------------------------------------
         <ns1:operations>
            <ns1:operator>ADD</ns1:operator>
            <ns1:Operation.Type>CampaignLabelOperation</ns1:Operation.Type>
            <ns1:operand>
               <ns1:campaignId>436647188</ns1:campaignId>
               <ns1:labelId>448280348</ns1:labelId>
            </ns1:operand>
         </ns1:operations>
      </ns1:mutateLabel>
   </ns0:Body>
</SOAP-ENV:Envelope>
````

will fail because https://github.com/googleads/googleads-python-lib/blob/master/googleads/adwords.py#L769
`return root.find('{http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/}Body').find(
     '%smutate' % self._adwords_namespace)`
will only look for `mutate` and not `mutateLabel`.

issue_title: Stop pypcap from capturing and writing network traffic to a file
issue_body:
I'm capturing network traffic with pypcap and writing it to a file with dpkt. Problem is I can't delete the file as I get an error saying it's still in use by another process. 

    seconds = 15
    starttime = datetime.datetime.now()

    pc = pcap.pcap()
    file = open('file.pcap', 'wb')
    pcapfile dpkt.pcap.Writer(file)

    for timestamp, packet in pc:
        currenttime = datetime.datetime.now()
        timedelta = currenttime - starttime
        if timedelta.seconds > seconds:
            break

        pcapfile.writepkt(packet,timestamp)

    file.close

    time.sleep(10)

    os.remove(file.pcap) = file in use by another process error


I'm currently overwriting the pcap file the next time I capture traffic. Doing this overwrites the file data most of the time, but occasionally it doesn't work and I end up with a large pcap file encompassing two sets of network capture data.  

How do you close the pcapy/dpkt process handle?

Also does anyone know a better way to stop pcapy capturing traffic after 15 seconds?

Thanks

issue_body: Thanks! :grin:

issue_title: remove version requirement for requests, or make it >= 2.11.1
issue_body: This causes unnecessary version conflicts for anything that loads a newer version of requests.

issue_title: Update requests to 1.11.x
issue_body:
Currently when running

```python
pip install googlemaps
```

I noticed it will uniintstal my ``request 1.11.x`` library and replace it with ``request 1.10`` library. Can we change this so this library uses the latest library?

issue_title: unit tests: create environment handler fixture
issue_body:
Currently the unit tests that depend on the pipeline module to generate the records, are implicitly depending on the test `tests/unit/test_pipelines.py::test_prepare_payload` to set some env variables for them before running (meaning that if you try to run them without running that one first, they will fail).

We should refactor it and add a fixture that properly sets up and cleans up the env variables (we might want mock.patch).

issue_body:

* [ ] collections
* [ ] _degree_type
* [ ] _rank

issue_title: Can't load instances which define hook specs
issue_body:
You currently can't instantiate a class which defines hook specs prior to loading

``` python
import pluggy

hookspec = pluggy.HookspecMarker('example')

class SpecSpace(object):
    @hookspec
    def myhook(self, stuff):
        """My custom hook spec.
        """

pluggy.PluginManager('example').add_hookspecs(SpecSpace())
```

because `pluggy.varnames()` doesn't successfully detect and remove the `self` name.

I realize this is an odd usage (and implied to be illegal based on the argname to `add_hookspecs()`) but it is conceivable. A contrived example might be where a user wishes to define the spec and the default hook implementation using the same function:

``` python
import pluggy

hookspec = pluggy.HookspecMarker('example')
hookimpl = pluggy.HookimplMarker('example')

class SpecSpace(object):
    def __init__(self, arg):
        self.arg = arg 

    @hookimpl
    @hookspec
    def hook1(self, stuff):
        """My custom hook spec.
        """
        if self.arg = 'doggy' and stuff == 'blah':
             # do this one thing
        else:
            # do this other thing

ss = SpecSpace('blah')
pm = pluggy.PluginManager('example')
pm.add_hookspecs(ss)
pm.register(ss)
```

I guess it's kind of silly...

Either way the implementation of `pluggy.varnames` is quite outdated and should be changed to more properly levarage the `inspect` module. If we prefer to not ever allow instances then a more explicit error should be thrown in `PluginManager.add_hookspecs()`

issue_body:
Hi,

Great job for pytest-html: I like it very much!

I am customizing the "**Environment**" part in my html report and it works fine when running tests one by one.

However, when using **pytest-xdist**, this part is set to the default 2 values: Platform and Python.

Is it a but or am I using the wrong implementation?

Thanks

This is the code I have in **conftest.py**
```
#==Retrieve Test Environment==#
def get_testenv():
	'''Retrieve Test Environment and store if for later use'''
	testenv_ini = "testenv.ini"
	testenv = ConfigParser.ConfigParser() 
	testenv.read(testenv_ini)

	if not testenv.has_option('TEST_ENV', '2.4 SCADE Display') \
		or testenv.get('TEST_ENV', '2.4 SCADE Display') != env.SDY_DIR:
		#Get EnvironmentError
		if not testenv.has_section('TEST_ENV'): testenv.add_section('TEST_ENV')
			
		#Hardware
		OS = subprocess.check_output('wmic os get BuildNumber, Caption, CSDVersion, OSArchitecture')
		RAM = [l.strip() for l in subprocess.check_output('wmic memorychip get capacity').splitlines() if re.match(r'\d', l)]
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '1.1 Machine', platform.node())
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '1.2 OS', re.sub(r'^.*OSArchitecture\s*(\d+)\s*(.*?)  (.*?)\s+((?:32|64)-bit)\s*$', r'\2 \4, \3, Build \1', OS))
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '1.3 Platform', platform.platform())
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '1.4 Model', re.sub(r'^\w+\s*(.*?)\s*$', r'\1', subprocess.check_output('wmic computersystem get model'))	)
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '1.5 RAM', sum([int(l)/1048576 for l in RAM]))
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '1.6 CPU', re.sub(r'^\w+\s*(.*?)\s*$', r'\1', subprocess.check_output('wmic cpu get name')))

		#Python and Diff
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '2.1 Python', platform.python_version())
		testenv.set('TEST_ENV', '2.2 GNU diff', subprocess.check_output('diff -v').splitlines()[0])
	
		with open(testenv_ini, 'w') as file:
			testenv.write(file)
				
	else:
		pass #Environment file up to date
		
	return testenv

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True) 
def _environment(request):
	'''Add extra information on Environment in html report'''
	testenv = get_testenv()

$ py.test -n2 test_foo.py
...
gw0 ok / gw1 C[gw0] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw0
gw2 C / gw1 ok[gw1] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw1
gw2 ok / gw3 C[gw2] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw2
gw4 C / gw3 ok[gw3] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw3
gw4 ok / gw5 C[gw4] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw4
gw6 C / gw5 ok[gw5] node down: Not properly terminated
...
```

Here pytest-xdist will be stuck trying to restart slaves forever.

By using `--max-slave-restart` it reaches the maximum but hangs anyway:

```
$ py.test -n2 test_foo.py --max-slave-restart=2
...
gw0 ok / gw1 C[gw0] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw0
gw2 C / gw1 ok[gw1] node down: Not properly terminated
Replacing crashed slave gw1
gw2 ok / gw3 C[gw2] node down: Not properly terminated
Maximum crashed slaves reached: 2
gw2 ok / gw3 ok[gw3] node down: Not properly terminated
Maximum crashed slaves reached: 2
(hangs)
```

This is a minimum example of an issue that came up at work, where one of the main modules of an application was causing a core dump on importing.

cc @gqmelo

I had my friend who never had streamlink installed (so never used the youtube API, on a residential IP, no VPN) try it just now on 2 random livestreams and he got the 403 forbidden on both URL's and whenever he retried the command, so if there is some kind of rate limiting/quota being hit it appears to be shared and not related to an individual's IP address.

For it to return the stream URL and play it 100% of the time

Any channel URL, preferably one with an active livestream happening. issue_title: Wrong health check object generated for COMMAND protocol
issue_body:
The current version of marathon-python (0.9.2) generates `COMMAND` type protocol healthchecks *command* property as a string, but current version of marathon expects an object with the 'value' property:

**Current:**
```json
{
  "protocol": "COMMAND",
  "command":  "curl -f -X GET http://$HOST:$PORT0/health"
}
```

**Expected:**
```json
{
  "protocol": "COMMAND",
  "command": { "value": "curl -f -X GET http://$HOST:$PORT0/health" }
}
```

My workaround was to create the health check like this:
```python
healthchecks.append(MarathonHealthCheck(
    protocol=hc.protocol,
    command={"value": hc.cmd}
))
```

I can make a pull request to address this, but there are some questions:

1. This should be fixed or we should assume the we have to send the object in the correct format? (:-1:)
2. This should be fixed, but there must be a quick test to see if the received command is already an object to avoid breaking the code of those who are creating the like I am?
3. Just change the code to create the format expected by marathon and let people fix if they bump into some error?

issue_title: Support UUID type in the sqlalchemy dialect
issue_body:
We are facing an issue with the cockroachdb sqlalchemy dialect. We use UUID types in our application but the dialect doesn't support it. Our current solution is to patch the dialect.py and add the UUID type imported from sqlalchemy to the _type_map like so:
`
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID

_type_map = dict(
    ...
    uuid=UUID,
)
`

issue_title: FeatureExtractor IDs in music21 don't match those in jSymbolic
issue_body: The [music21 documentation](http://web.mit.edu/music21/doc/moduleReference/moduleFeaturesJSymbolic.html) lists IDs of the feature extractors in a list. This differs from the list given in jSymbolic, for instance in the [following explanation of features](http://jmir.sourceforge.net/manuals/jSymbolic_manual/featureexplanations_files/featureexplanations.html). Especially because some music21 feature extractor names are different from the corresponding jSymbolic ones (for reasons I do not know), having the IDs match may be desirable.

issue_title: cannot generate lilypond code from a triplet of chords
issue_body:
The following code demonstrates the problem.
It depends on the current master of python-ly (https://github.com/wbsoft/python-ly) for converting lilypond to musicxml.
(Using master of python-ly is important in this case, because very recently a bug related to parsing triplets of chords was fixed)
```
import ly.musicxml
import music21


class Lily2Stream(object):
    """
    class that converts simple lilypond fragments (containing only notes,rests and chords)
    to music21 streams
    """
    def __init__(self):
        pass

    @staticmethod
    def parse(lytext=""):
        """
        convert from lilypond to stream
        :param lytext: string containing simple lilypond fragment, e.g. "{ a b c }"
        :return: music21 stream
        """
        e = ly.musicxml.writer()
        e.parse_text(lytext)
        xml = e.musicxml()
        m = xml.tostring().decode("utf-8")
        s = music21.converter.parse(m, format="musicxml")
        return s

    @staticmethod
    def unparse(stream):
        """
        convert from music21 stream to lilypond fragment
        :param stream
        :return: string containing lilypond code fragment
        """
        lpc = music21.lily.translate.LilypondConverter()
        lp_music_list = music21.lily.lilyObjects.LyMusicList()
        lpc.context = lp_music_list
        lpc.appendObjectsToContextFromStream(stream)
        return str(lpc.context)

if __name__ == "__main__":

    # first triplet of single notes -> these work well
    l = Lily2Stream()
    s = l.parse("{ \\times2/3 { a8 b c } }")
    s.show("txt")

# looks ok:
#{0.0} <music21.metadata.Metadata object at 0x7feb9e3e42e8>
#{0.0} <music21.stream.Part 0x7feb9e3e4668>
#    {0.0} <music21.instrument.Instrument P1: >
#    {0.0} <music21.stream.Measure 1 offset=0.0>
#        {0.0} <music21.clef.TrebleClef>
#        {0.0} <music21.meter.TimeSignature 4/4>
#        {0.0} <music21.note.Note A>
#        {0.3333} <music21.note.Note B>
#        {0.6667} <music21.note.Note C>

    s.show("musicxml") # looks ok
    print(l.unparse(s))
# looks ok:
# \new Staff  = xawweyfawxbxyzy { \partial 32*8
#      \clef "treble"
#      \time 4/4
#      \times 2/3 { a 8
#         b 8
#         c 8
#          }
#
#      \bar "|"  %{ end measure 1 %}
#       }

    # now triplet of chords -> these do not serialize to lilypond correctly
    # (but they do serialize to musicxml correctly)
    s = l.parse("{ \\times2/3 { <a c>8 <a b> <a c> } }")
    s.show("txt")

#looks ok:
#{0.0} <music21.metadata.Metadata object at 0x7feb9be4e390>
#{0.0} <music21.stream.Part 0x7feb9be4ea90>
#    {0.0} <music21.instrument.Instrument P1: >
#    {0.0} <music21.stream.Measure 1 offset=0.0>
#        {0.0} <music21.clef.TrebleClef>
#        {0.0} <music21.meter.TimeSignature 4/4>
#        {0.0} <music21.chord.Chord A3 C3>
#        {0.3333} <music21.chord.Chord A3 B3>
#        {0.6667} <music21.chord.Chord A3 C3>

    s.show("musicxml") # looks ok
    print(l.unparse(s))

#wrong: triplet lost
#\new Staff  = xawweyfzfedaaaw { \partial 32*8
#      \clef "treble"
#      \time 4/4
#      < a  c  > 8
#      < a  b  > 8
#      < a  c  > 8
#      \bar "|"  %{ end measure 1 %}
#       }
```

issue_title: Error creating new registration - Internal error caused by email domain
issue_body:
I'm getting the error below when trying to generate new certificates consistently when using emails for the finergy.com.au domain. It works, if I use a different email domain. I suspect there is something different on this domain configuration that is causing the internal error but I can't tell what's the issue from the error message. 

The domain has an A and MX record as per below:
```
host finergy.com.au
finergy.com.au has address 103.230.157.51
finergy.com.au has IPv6 address 2404:9400:1:0:216:3eff:fef0:18fb
finergy.com.au mail is handled by 1 finergy-com-au.mail.protection.outlook.com
```
Error message:
```
2016-02-09 02:10:32,053:DEBUG:root:Received <Response [500]>. Headers: {'Content-Length': '96', 'Expires': 'Tue, 09 Feb 2016 02:10:32 GMT', 'Server': 'nginx', 'Connection': 'close', 'Pragma': 'no-cache', 'Cache-Control': 'max-age=0, no-cache, no-store', 'Date': 'Tue, 09 Feb 2016 02:10:32 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'application/problem+json', 'Replay-Nonce': 'K0GwryFnl1B0ZFmXpoA5WV6AsHfOYYnb7htoEcPR37Y'}. Content: '{"type":"urn:acme:error:serverInternal","detail":"Error creating new registration","status":500}'
```
Looks like it's the same or similar error [this person](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/registration-fails-error-creating-new-registration-500-when-email-address-has-a-in-it/5384) is seeing too.

issue_title: "letsencrypt -h topic" broken
issue_body:
As noticed by @bmw in https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/pull/835#discussion_r40501773, `letsencrypt -h topic` is broken:

```
$ letsencrypt -h nginx
usage: letsencrypt [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [domains]

The Let's Encrypt agent can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By
default, it will attempt to use a webserver both for obtaining and installing
the cert. run
       [-h]

Obtain a certificate and install.

optional arguments:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
$ letsencrypt --help nginx
usage: 
  letsencrypt [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [domains]

The Let's Encrypt agent can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By
default, it will attempt to use a webserver both for obtaining and installing
the cert.  

positional arguments:
  SUBCOMMAND
    run                 Obtain a certificate and install.
    auth                Authenticate & obtain cert, but do not install it.
    install             Install a previously obtained cert in a server.
    revoke              Revoke a previously obtained certificate.
    rollback            Rollback server configuration changes made during
                        install.
    config_changes      Show changes made to server config during installation
    plugins             List server software plugins.

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -c CONFIG_FILE, --config CONFIG_FILE
                        config file path (default: None)

nginx:
  Nginx Web Server

  --nginx-server-root NGINX_SERVER_ROOT
                        Nginx server root directory. (default: /etc/nginx)
  --nginx-ctl NGINX_CTL
                        Path to the 'nginx' binary, used for 'configtest' and
                        retrieving nginx version number. (default: nginx)

```

I'm on 523dba0b999ec46daf8c74edbfe1c29a3b43d8d2.

issue_title: log changes to Apache conf files right before performing .reload()
issue_body: This should make it much easier to see what's going wrong in cases like #1994 where the changes we think we should have made to conf files apparently aren't quite what's happened.

issue_title: Report certificate creation explicitly
issue_body:
Users are confused about whether the client actually succeeded in issuing the certificate, see https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/issues/734#issuecomment-135960602.

As suggested by @centminmod We could additionally report:
```
- your SSL certificates have been created at /etc/letsencrypt/live/le1.http2ssl.xyz
```

@bmw, would you like to take it?

issue_title: Get agent vars dataframe slow/silently crashes when number of agents is high
issue_body: I want to run my model with 100K agents, and save to a csv so I can do some data analysis on them. However, the get_agents_vars_dataframe function is either very slow (left cooking over night, still busy by lunch the day after) or it has silently crashed. Has anyone encountered this before, or have a suggested solution?

issue_title: Setting iterations greater than 1 in Batchrunner has no effect
issue_body:
If the iteration parameter for a Batchrunner is greater than 1, the model will immediately reach max_steps after one run of a parameter combination. This is because the model is initialised once per parameter combination.  See code below (from run_all() in batchrunner.py):          

```
            for param_values in product(*param_ranges):
                kwargs = dict(zip(param_names, param_values))
                kwargs.update(self.fixed_parameters)
                model = self.model_cls(**kwargs)

                for _ in range(self.iterations):
                    self.run_model(model)
                    # Collect and store results:
```

I think moving the model initialisation to the inner loop would be a solution to this problem. See code below:

```
            for param_values in product(*param_ranges):
                kwargs = dict(zip(param_names, param_values))
                kwargs.update(self.fixed_parameters)

                for _ in range(self.iterations):
                    model = self.model_cls(**kwargs)
                    self.run_model(model)
                    # Collect and store results:
```

issue_title: (some?) settings not being applied with custom date_formats
issue_body:
- version: 0.7.0

I have the following case:

This is the expected behaviour of the setting `'PREFER_DAY_OF_MONTH'` without using personalized `date_formats`:
```python3
dateparser.parse('august 2016')
# >> datetime.datetime(2016, 8, 19, 0, 0)
dateparser.parse('august 2016', settings={'PREFER_DAY_OF_MONTH': 'first')
# >> datetime.datetime(2016, 8, 1, 0, 0)
```
Now let's say we want to parse "august-16" as "august of 2016", so we use the custom format `"%B-%y"`. We obtain the right behaviour:
```python3
dateparser.parse('august-16', date_formats=['%B-%y'])
# >> datetime.datetime(2016, 8, 31, 0, 0)
```
And now let's use the setting `'PREFER_DAY_OF_MONTH'` along with the `date_formats`:
```python3
dateparser.parse('august-16', date_formats=['%B-%y'], settings={'PREFER_DAY_OF_MONTH': 'first'})
# >> datetime.datetime(2016, 8, 31, 0, 0)
```

In this case, `dateparser.parser` has ignored the settings and returned the last day of month instead of the first one.`.

issue_title: Solidity function name collision
issue_body:
### Manticore version
https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/tree/61babdbe21b99c93c74d7ccecbf520108071761a

### Summary of the problem
If two function share the same name but with a different signature, Manticore will not be able to execute of the two functions.

### Step to reproduce the behavior
```python
from manticore.ethereum import ManticoreEVM
from manticore.core.smtlib import Operators, solver

m = ManticoreEVM() # initiate the blockchain
source_code = '''
contract Test{

    function ret(uint) returns(uint){
        return 1;
    }

    function ret(uint, uint) returns(uint){
        return 2;
    }
}
'''

# Generate the accounts
user_account = m.create_account(balance=1000)
contract_account = m.solidity_create_contract(source_code, owner=user_account)

# Try to execute ret(uint), but instead Manticore executes ret(uint,uint) 
contract_account.ret(m.SValue)

print m.workspace

m.finalize()
```

`mcore_XXXX/test_00000000.tx` will then contain:
```
Function call:
ret(0,0) -> RETURN (*)
return: 2 
```

### Expected behavior
The execution of `ret(uint)`. 
`test_00000000.tx` should contain:
```
Function call:
ret(0) -> RETURN (*)
return: 1
```

### Actual behavior
The execution of `ret(uint, uint)`

issue_title: Best way of finding if a page exists at a path ?
issue_body:
In a ClassBasedView I'm trying to determine if a page exists at a path (before I redirect to it) - whats the best way of doing this supporting different language slugs ?

So far I have this, which doesn't take different language slugs into account:
```
    def page_exists(self, path):
        path_components = [
            component for component in path.split('/') if component
        ]
        try:
            # Check validity by attempting to fetch page
            request = self.request
            page, args, kwargs = \
                request.site.root_page.specific.route(request, path_components[1:])  # noqa

            return True
        except Http404:
            pass

        return False
```

issue_title: `SimilarityTransform.estimate` returns unstable, wrong value
issue_body:
## Description

It appears that `SimilarityTransform.estimate` is returning very wrong answers for over-determined data. In this case, the data does not lead to a well-fitting similarity transform. But the offsets of ~1e17 in the translation parameters of the transformation matrix are very wrong.

```python
import numpy as np
import skimage.transform

desired_points = np.array([[  0.75,  10.  ],
       [  0.75,  10.5 ],
       [  1.25,  10.5 ],
       [  1.25,  10.  ],
       [  0.75,  10.  ],
       [  0.75,  10.5 ],
       [  1.25,  10.5 ],
       [  1.25,  10.  ]])

actual_points = np.array([[3066,  344],
       [3066,  144],
       [2864,  144],
       [2864,  346],
       [2422,  348],
       [2422,  146],
       [2220,  150],
       [2222,  350]])

t = skimage.transform.SimilarityTransform()
t.estimate(dst=actual_points, src=desired_points)
t.params
```

On one machine 
I get 
```python
array([[ -8.67142009e+02,   6.46331840e+01,  -2.19998003e+17],
       [ -6.46331840e+01,  -8.67142009e+02,  -4.25662457e+16],
       [  0.00000000e+00,   0.00000000e+00,   1.00000000e+00]])
```

on another
```python
array([[  0.00000000e+00,   3.47821456e+02,   1.14356273e+18],
       [ -3.47821456e+02,   0.00000000e+00,  -8.88987484e+18],
       [  0.00000000e+00,   0.00000000e+00,   1.00000000e+00]])
```

in both cases, the `fit` call returns `True` 



## Way to reproduce
One machine:
```
In [83]: sys.version

issue_title: deprecation_reason not works for Mutation.Argument
issue_body:
Similar as #368 
When I set `deprecation_reason` on a Argument, it raises an `TypeError` and says `deprecation_reason` is a unexpected keyword argument.

Environment:
Mac/Docker container with Ubuntu image.
python: 3.6.5
graphene: 2.1.2

Reproduce snippet:

```Python
import graphene


class CreateSomething(graphene.Mutation):

    class Arguments:
        deprecated_id = graphene.ID(deprecation_reason='deprecated')

    ok = graphene.Boolean()

    @staticmethod
    def mutate(root, *_, **__):
        return CreateSomething(ok=True)


class RootMutation(graphene.ObjectType):
    create_something = CreateSomething.Field()


schema = graphene.Schema(mutation=RootMutation)

query = '''
mutation createSomething($deprecatedId: ID) {
    createSomething(deprecatedId: $deprecatedId) {
        ok
    }
}
'''
result = schema.execute(
    query, variable_values={
        'deprecatedId': '123',
    })
print('result:', result.data)

```

Expected Result:

we can get this result by removing `deprecation_reason` keyword argument.

```
result: OrderedDict([('createSomething', OrderedDict([('ok', True)]))])
```

What happens:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "my_app.py", line 16, in <module>
    class RootMutation(graphene.ObjectType):
  File "my_app.py", line 17, in RootMutation
    create_something = CreateSomething.Field()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/mutation.py", line 87, in Field
    required=required,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/field.py", line 53, in __init__
    self.args = to_arguments(args or OrderedDict(), extra_args)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/argument.py", line 55, in to_arguments
    arg = Argument.mounted(arg)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/mountedtype.py", line 20, in mounted
    **unmounted.kwargs
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'deprecation_reason'
```

issue_title: Where to limit the balance proof's `transferred_amount` - overflow at settlement
issue_body:
A very big `transferred_amount` can cause an overflow when calculating the settlement amounts. this will cause the participant's tokens to be sent to the other participant.

The issue is where to set a limit to `transferred_amount`, which is going to be relative to the `deposit`.
My proposal is to only set this limit in the Raiden and not in the smart contract.

Reasons:
- `deposit` value can be very different per token
- Raiden client has access to all variables needed to set this limit dynamically
- Raiden client is the official client to be used. Using anything else of course poses a risk

Discussions:
https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-contracts/pull/114#discussion_r194008259

issue_title: implement `graceful shutdown` logic
issue_body:
There seem to be three modes of shutdown we want to support:
- shutdown and leave: needs a "settle all" and then just shutdown
- ~shutdown and keep channels open "3rd party challenging"~
- unintentional shutdown and resync #69 #2 

For cleanly shutting down a node, there needs to be a shutdown process, that:
- [ ] settles open channels *or* wait for all pending transfers to complete

issue_title: If Dataset transforms to another Dataset with more elements, the opimimiter cannot infer the correct number of elements in each Epoch.]
issue_body:
If the user create an Distributed DataSet and then use transformers to transform the rdd to a rdd with more elements, then put the DataSet to a ptimizer, the optimizer can not automatically inter the correct number of elements in each epoch. Here a simple example is given as follows:

```scala
case class Tokenizer(sep: String) extends Transformer[String, Seq[String]] 

// flatten the given iterator
case class Flatten() extends Transformer[Seq[String], String] {
  override def apply(prev: Iterator[Seq[String]]): Iterator[String] =
    prev.flatten
}

val trainSet = DataSet.rdd(sc.textFile(params.trainDataLocation)) -> Tokenizer() -> Flatten() -> ToBatch(batchSize)

val optimizer = Optimizer(
      model = model,
      dataset = trainSet,
      criterion =  ... )
```

In the above case, assume the lines in the given input text file is 100, then it was transformed into 1000 words. However, during the training process, the optimizer still regards the size of training set is 100 in each epoch. It is better to enhance the function to meet this requirement.

For now, the solution can be that:
1. Transform the DataSet to RDD and then map the transformer
2.  Transform the result RDD to DataSet and then transform to Batch.

code example as follows:
```
val trainSetRDD = DataSet.rdd(sc.textFile(params.trainDataLocation)
      .toDistributed()
      .data(false)
       .mapPartitions[String]((Tokenizer() -> Flatten()).apply)

val trainSet = DataSet.rdd(trainSetRDD) -> ToBatch(batchSize)
```

$ inveniomanage instance create test
/Users/lnielsen/src/invenio-base/invenio_base/cli.py:45: UserWarning: This command have been deprecated.
  warnings.warn('This command have been deprecated.')
This command have been deprecated. Please use this instead:

pip install cookiecutter
cookiecutter https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-instance
```

Note, there's also a spelling mistake above which should also be fixed: ``This command have been`` -> ``This command has been``.

Probably removing the section is the best, since this should be documented in general Invenio documentation instead.

issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: API endpoint to delete content DB from file system
issue_body:
In the current implementation on `import-export`, we use the `ChannelResource` to 'delete' a Channel from the server:

```
// manageContentActions.js line 48
ChannelResource.getModel(channelId).delete()
```

This deletes the Channel's metadata, but not the files comprising the Channel. Apparently, this means if the server is restarted, the Channel metadata will be reconstructed and reappear in the app.

Assuming that 'to delete' means to disable the the channel _and_ remove its contents from the device, we need an API to do the later.

An alternative interpretation of 'to delete' is to simply disable the channel but _not_ remove its contents from the device. This would probably also need another API.

issue_title: Provide positional information in errors dictionary when serializing a collection (many=True)
issue_body:
When validating a collection with invalid elements, the returned errors dictionary does not provide any information about the positions of invalid elements

Example:

```python
def validate_age(age):
    if age < 21:
        raise ValidationError('Must be over 21')

class UserSchema(Schema):
    age = fields.Number(validate=validate_age)

in_data = [{'age': 24}, {'age': 19}]
data, errors = UserSchema(many=True).load(in_data)
```

### Current

```python
errors  # => {'age': ['Must be over 21']}
```

### Possible solution

Provide positional information in a nested dictionary, keyed by index.

```python
errors  # => {'age': {1: ['Must be over 21']}}
```

issue_title: Custom SQLAlchemy types with a python_type that is not a class fail on init
issue_body:
Let me start by saying this might not be a bug.

Looks like https://github.com/kelvinhammond/marshmallow-sqlalchemy/commit/12da838c8ca3e02516dd83852cef870d1d596b1b breaks compatibility with sqlalchemy-utils (particularly `PhoneNumberType`). It seems like a bug in sqlalchemy-utils where it has a `python_type` of `method` (rather than a class).

I have a pull request which should fix the issue:
kvesteri/sqlalchemy-utils#248

I think this is a bug in sqlalchemy-utils, but it might also be reasonable to be a little more forgiving by gracefully handling cases where `issubclass` will fail. Tough call - on the one hand it is good to be accommodating but on the other hand we should trust that people are adhering to what appears to be a pretty definitive interface definition:

http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/core/type_api.html#sqlalchemy.types.TypeEngine.python_type

Here is the relevant stack trace:

```python
  File "marshmallow_sqlalchemy/convert.py", line 219, in _add_column_kwargs
    if not python_type or not issubclass(python_type, uuid.UUID):
TypeError: issubclass() arg 1 must be a class

(Pdb) python_type
<bound method PhoneNumberType.python_type of PhoneNumberType(length=20)>
```

issue_title: Incorrect value of magnification to conserve surface brightness after applying shear in Demo 6.
issue_body:
I was going through demo6 recently, and it seems that the value of the magnification applied to conserve surface brightness after the application of shear is incorrect.

The specific lines I'm referring to are:

```
shear : 
        type : G1G2 
        g1 : -0.027
        g2 : 0.031

# Also apply a magnification mu = ( (1-kappa)^2 - |gamma|^2 )^-1
# This conserves surface brightness, so it scales both the area and flux.
magnify : 1.082

```

For the same value of shear, the 'mu' (or magnification) that I get from my calculation is 1.0017 instead of 1.082 as mentioned in the example. To confirm this, I ran a small test to compare the value I calculated against the value provided in the example in their ability to conserve the surface brightness, and it appears that my number performs better than the one given in the example. 

The details of the test are:
- Construct a Gaussian galaxy and calculate it's surface brightness (true surface brightness).
- Shear the above Gaussian galaxy.
- Apply the magnification I calculate to a copy of the sheared galaxy, and calculate it's surface brightness (calculated surface brightness).
- Apply the magnification given in the example to another copy of the sheared galaxy and calculate it's surface brightness (example surface brightness).

And the code is:
```
# Magnification Check

import galsim
import numpy as np

image_size = 64

images = []
for i in range(3):
	images.append(galsim.Image(image_size, image_size))

gal_flux = 1.e5    # total counts on the image
gal_sigma = 3.     # arcsec

pixel_scale = 0.2

sb_factor = (image_size ** 2) * pixel_scale

gal = galsim.Gaussian(flux=gal_flux, sigma=gal_sigma)

# Drawing Image
gal.drawImage(image = images[0], scale=pixel_scale)

# Calculate Surface Brightness of the Non-Sheared Galaxy
print "Actual Surface Brightness :::"
print np.sum(images[0].array)/sb_factor


gal_sh = gal.shear(g1 = -0.027, g2 = 0.031)

# Apply magnification to conserve surface brightness after applying shear

# Calculated
gal_cal = gal_sh.magnify(1.0017)
# Drawing Image
gal_cal.drawImage(image = images[1], scale=pixel_scale)

# Example
gal_ex = gal_sh.magnify(1.082)
# Drawing Image
gal_ex.drawImage(image = images[2], scale=pixel_scale)

# Calculate Surface Brightness of the Sheared Galaxy
print "Calc Mag Surface Brightness :::"
print np.sum(images[1].array)/sb_factor

print "Example Mag Surface Brightness :::"
print np.sum(images[2].array)/sb_factor
```

The numbers that I obtain are:

True Surface Brightness: 114.17
Calculated Surface Brightness: 114.24
Example Surface Brightness: 121.55

which says that my calculated number performs better than the one given in the example. 

Should a correction be made?

I had tried to use error reporting module to trace my errors of my system. I found that http_context is not reported. After I check the document of stack driver error reporting, I found there is a bug there. The fix is simple. I could submit a PR latter. The code to reproduce is list below, ```python from google.cloud import error_reporting cli = error_reporting.Client(service='python_test') context = error_reporting.client.HTTPContext( method='GET', url='http://test.com', user_agent='Chrome', referrer='http://referer.com', response_status_code=400, remote_ip= '8.8.8.8', ) cli.report('error report test', http_context=context) ``` Environment 
OS: MAC
Python 2.7.10
google-cloud-error-reporting==0.22.0

issue_title: blob.download_to_filename fails with google-cloud-storage==1.3.0
issue_body:
Due to the notable implementation changes in **google-cloud-storage 1.3.0**, the **_blob.download_to_filename_** fails whatever **google-cloud** from **0.24.0** to **0.27.0** (latest)

Our prod server which using **google-cloud==0.24.0** has been broken since last Saturday. I analyzed a bit the error. The problem is come from the upgraded package **google-cloud-storage==1.3.0**. We are using the **google-cloud-storage==1.2.0**.

I did several tests which figured out the following problems:

**Problem 1:** _blob.download_to_filename failed when using google-cloud==0.27.0_ (included **google-cloud-storage==1.3.0** as default, defined in **setup.py**). Here is a part of stacktrace:
`blob.download_to_filename(blob_local_path)
  File "/root/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 482, in download_to_filename
    self.download_to_file(file_obj, client=client)
  File "/root/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 464, in download_to_file
    self._do_download(transport, file_obj, download_url, headers)
  File "/root/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 418, in _do_download
    download.consume(transport)
  File "/root/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/resumable_media/requests/download.py", line 101, in consume
    self._write_to_stream(result)
  File "/root/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/resumable_media/requests/download.py", line 62, in _write_to_stream
    with response:
AttributeError: __exit__
`

**Problem 2:** _blob.download_to_filename failed when using google-cloud==0.24.0_ (included **google-cloud-storage==1.3.0** as default, defined in **setup.py**). Here is a part of stacktrace:
`  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 482, in download_to_filename
    self.download_to_file(file_obj, client=client)
  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 464, in download_to_file
    self._do_download(transport, file_obj, download_url, headers)
  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/storage/blob.py", line 418, in _do_download
    download.consume(transport)
  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/resumable_media/requests/download.py", line 96, in consume
    transport, method, url, **request_kwargs)
  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/resumable_media/requests/_helpers.py", line 101, in http_request
    func, RequestsMixin._get_status_code, retry_strategy)
  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/resumable_media/_helpers.py", line 146, in wait_and_retry
    response = func()
  File "dev_env/python_venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google_auth_httplib2.py", line 198, in request
    uri, method, body=body, headers=request_headers, **kwargs)
TypeError: request() got an unexpected keyword argument 'data'`

So, why it happened? I realized that in the _REQUIREMENTS_ of the package **google-cloud** which is defined in the file **setup.py**. An example for **google-cloud==0.27.0** https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/setup.py#L67

- **google-cloud==0.24.0** requires google-cloud-storage >= 1.0.0 and **< 2.0dev**
- **google-cloud==0.25.0** requires google-cloud-storage >= 1.1.0 and **< 2.0dev**  => https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/commit/bd9fdfba07fac63a91847628613928a569250c0f#diff-2eeaed663bd0d25b7e608891384b7298R66
- **google-cloud==0.25.1** requires google-cloud-storage >= 1.1.0 and **< 1.2dev**  => https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/commit/ad4b02c88088666e298aa53cd61687b461c09191#diff-2eeaed663bd0d25b7e608891384b7298R66
- **google-cloud==0.26.0** requires google-cloud-storage >= 1.2.0 and **< 2.0dev** => https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/commit/214aba604fbcaa9e4936fa1798efde050389992a#diff-2eeaed663bd0d25b7e608891384b7298R67
- **google-cloud==0.26.1** requires google-cloud-storage >= 1.2.0 and **< 1.3dev** => https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/commit/ea5245ad27e777c654448a259a9fa5f6bf5d758e#diff-2eeaed663bd0d25b7e608891384b7298R67
- **google-cloud==0.27.0** requires google-cloud-storage >= 1.3.0 and **< 1.4dev** => https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/setup.py#L67

We can see that if we only define **google-cloud==[0.24.0 to 0.27.0]**, "pip install" always try to install **google-cloud-storage 1.3.0** which requires the dependencies **google-cloud-core ~= 0.26**. That's why the bug happens.

**Solution:**
- Downgrade to **google-cloud < 0.27.0** and force the requirements **google-cloud-storage < 1.3.0**
- The problem seems still there with **google-cloud==0.27.0** and **google-cloud-storage==1.3.0** (to check but at least it has been broken for my case, see **Problem 2** above)

**Suggestion:** better manage the deps for **google-cloud** in **setup.py**.

Thanks.

issue_title: Pubsub stops receiving messages
issue_body:
I have storage as my publisher and a subscriber on the compute engine as the receiver. 
Below is the subscriber code that I use.
```
def receive_messages_with_flow_control(project, subscription_name):
    """Receives messages from a pull subscription with flow control."""
    subscriber = pubsub_v1.SubscriberClient()
    subscription_path = subscriber.subscription_path(
        project, subscription_name)
    idocStorageAbsolutePath = config.idocsStorageAbsolutePath
    makeDirIfNotExist(idocStorageAbsolutePath)
    def callback(message):
        storage_data = message.data
        process_data(storage_data,idocStorageAbsolutePath)
        message.ack()

    # Limit the subscriber to only have ten outstanding messages at a time.
    flow_control = pubsub_v1.types.FlowControl(max_messages=50)
    subscriber.subscribe(
        subscription_path, callback=callback, flow_control=flow_control)

    # The subscriber is non-blocking, so we must keep the main thread from
    # exiting to allow it to process messages in the background.
    logger.info('Listening for messages on {}'.format(subscription_path))
    while True:
        time.sleep(5)
```
After a day or two of the subscriber running, it stops consuming the messages. I have to manually restart it to make it work again.  
Please suggest what I am doing wrong.

PS: The data flowing speed into the topic is high during the day and reduces at night.

issue_title: Storage: add support for Per-Object Storage Class
issue_body:
We need to add support for Per-Object Storage Class (POSC).

Here some extra information to help:

- [POSC info from Cloud Storage documentation](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/per-object-storage-class)
- [POSC example with JSON API](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/changing-storage-classes)
- [Storage Class info from Cloud Storage documentation](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/storage-classes)

On top of that, google-cloud-dotnet has made an implementation which can be used as reference. 

- [Please view the Pull Request to understand the changes](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-dotnet/pull/766)

If you have any implementation questions, please direct them at jskeet

/cc @jskeet @danoscarmike

issue_title: Is it possible to add nRF5x dfu with linux BLE driver?
issue_body:
I have few puck.js devices (it is nRF52 based board) and want to update firmware. I have not any Android 6 device so I can not use Android tools (I've checked all phones I can access - nRF tools not supported).
At the same time, I have few linux boards with BLE supported. I can see my puck.js devices, for example:
` hcitool lescan`
`LE Scan ...`
`DC:0F:DF:1E:D4:73 DfuTarg`
I'd want to update nRF firmware using the linux board. Is it possible?
Unfortunately, Python is not in my skills. I'm mostly low-level programmer (and so I can connect puck.js to JTAG/SWD and update firmware - but it seems to be ugly way).

issue_title: Update copyright information on website
issue_body: Just a trivial one, your website (http://gitpython.readthedocs.org/) has a copyright notice at the bottom that is out of date. Updating it might helpful so that people don't mistakenly get the impression that this software is not supported anymore.

issue_title: Fails on import with custom exception
issue_body:
On Windows, without git available on path, the following happens on import of GitPython:
```python
[package stack]
    from git.exc import GitError
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\__init__.py", line 45, in <module>
    from git.repo import Repo               # @NoMove @IgnorePep8
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\repo\__init__.py", line 4, in <module>
    from .base import *
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\repo\base.py", line 31, in <module>
    from git.remote import Remote, add_progress, to_progress_instance
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\remote.py", line 190, in <module>
    class FetchInfo(object):
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\remote.py", line 219, in FetchInfo
    v = Git().version_info[:2]
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 461, in version_info
    return self._version_info
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 424, in __getattr__
    return LazyMixin.__getattr__(self, name)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\gitdb\util.py", line 256, in __getattr__
    self._set_cache_(attr)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 444, in _set_cache_
    version_numbers = self._call_process('version').split(' ')[2]
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 877, in _call_process
    return self.execute(call, **exec_kwargs)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 602, in execute
    raise GitCommandNotFound(command, err)
git.exc.GitCommandNotFound: Cmd('git') not found due to: FileNotFoundError('[WinError 2] The system cannot find the file specified')
  cmdline: git version
```

Since it fails while trying to import the exception type that it raises (`GitError`), the only way to catch the error is with a `try: ... except Exception:` block, which is needlessly wide. It would be nice if a better pattern was available for conditional use of the package.

issue_title: geo_json data_out path issue
issue_body:
I want both the `create_map`'s html and the `data_out`'s json file to be created in `maps/` folder instead of the current working directory. Currently this is not possible because when I `data_out = 'maps/xyz.json'` and also `create_map(path='maps/xyz.html')`, the produced html source has `.defer(d3.json, 'maps/xyz.json')`. Instead it should've been `.defer(d3.json, 'xyz.json')` since they are both in `maps/`.

So, my suggestion to fix this issue is that folium should consider the `data_out`'s path, relative to `create_html`'s path. To achieve this, the path passed to `create_html` needs to be parsed and the generated json file should be put accordingly. So, if the `data_out` has no path (but only a filename_ then it should be in the same directory where html file is generated.

issue_title: Redundant popup_on?
issue_body:
What's the reason for having the parameter `popup_on` on features? In which cases is it useful? To me it seems sensible that if a popup text is passed one wants a popup and otherwise it should be off.

At the very least we should enable a popup if text is passed and disable the popup if no text is passed. Provided that it is useful, the parameter can be maintained to force behaviour (for example showing popup when no popup text is passed and, vice versa, hiding even if text is passed).

issue_title: Missing support to STDIN
issue_body: It can be useful providing a string to stdin, in particular with Subprocess.

issue_body:
The value should be:
"opencensus-java [" + $VERSION + "]";

issue_title: Disable colors for junit reports
issue_body:
We use pytest and nbval to generate junit-xml files. Unfortunately there is no option to disable the coloring of output messages. Here is an example junit xml file:

```

issue_title: Add Sample Output to Examples in Comments
issue_body:
Hello, 
I was walking through the examples today (i.e. https://github.com/watson-developer-cloud/python-sdk/blob/master/examples/relationship_extraction_v1_beta.py ) and noticed that they don't give a lot of insight into what's going on in the example, since it's just an import/invoke. 

Since all of these scripts require login credentials I think it'd be a nice addition if the output from the print statements at the end of the script could be followed by comments showing what the output would be. For example:

```
print(["Hi!" for i in range(0, 3)])
"""
['Hi!', 'Hi!', 'Hi!']
"""
```

issue_title: Searching in DecimalField
issue_body:
I have a column with values defined as DecimalField and filtering by search term is not working for them.

I think the problem might be that for both FloatField and DecimalField you pass to the query a float value: https://github.com/pivotal-energy-solutions/django-datatable-view/blob/master/datatableview/views.py#L179.
